acceptance_criteria: `Invoke-PolyphonySdlc.ps1 -ApexId N -Intent new` against a work item in state `Done`, `Closed`, or `Removed` exits non-zero before any conductor invocation.
The refusal message names the state and points to both `reopen` and `polyphony reset --root-id N` as remediation paths.
A Pester test in `scripts/` covers each terminal state (`Done`, `Closed`, `Removed`) and one non-terminal control case (e.g. `Active`).
Behaviour is unchanged for non-terminal states — no regression to the happy path.
description:
Make `Invoke-PolyphonySdlc.ps1` refuse to dispatch when the target work item is in a terminal state (`Done`, `Closed`, `Removed`). Surface a clear remediation message: "AB#N is in state Done. Reopen or run `polyphony reset --root-id N` to start fresh." Highest-leverage item in the Epic — ~30 lines in [scripts/Invoke-PolyphonySdlc.ps1](scripts/Invoke-PolyphonySdlc.ps1) plus a Pester test. Mirrors AB#3085's launcher-rework pattern. Ship as a hand-built PR before the rest of the fleet so re-dispatch on a closed item stops being an immediate footgun.

The refusal must be explicit and operator-visible — not a silent reject. The remediation message is the contract: it must mention both `reopen` and `polyphony reset --root-id N` so the operator has a clear next step regardless of intent.
title: Apex-driver pre-flight refusal on terminal work-item state

acceptance_criteria: `polyphony reset --root-id N` strips ADO tags matching the `PolyphonyTag` DU, archives + clears polyphony-authored comments, deletes per-root state dir, deletes per-root branches local + remote, and removes per-run worktrees.
A typed `PolyphonyTag` discriminated union exists with state, intent, and facet variants and is the sole source of truth for which tags the reset verb recognizes.
`--dry-run` enumerates every artifact that would be touched without performing any mutation.
Confirmation gate prompts before any destructive action by default; `--force` skips the prompt.
Verb has a JSON output contract test under `tests/` consistent with existing verbs.
Verb is documented (README or `docs/`) including the remediation pattern referenced by the Issue 1 refusal message.
description:
Add a new CLI verb `polyphony reset --root-id N` that scrubs all polyphony-authored state for a single root, so the operator has a one-shot path to put a work item back into a re-dispatchable shape.

Scope of scrub:
- ADO tags matching the `PolyphonyTag` DU below (strip from the work item).
- Polyphony-authored ADO comments (archive to a sidecar artifact, then clear).
- Per-root state dir at `<git-common-dir>/polyphony/N/`.
- Per-root branches both local and remote: `feature/N`, `plan/N*`, `impl/N-*`, `mg/N_*`, `sdlc/apex/N`.
- Per-run worktrees under `polyphony-runs/apex-N/`.

Introduce a typed `PolyphonyTag` discriminated union (state vs intent vs facet variants) as the source of truth for which tags polyphony owns. The reset verb consumes it; downstream items (Issue 3 in particular) consume it too. Keep the DU minimal — exactly the variants needed today; do not pre-expand into a full tag taxonomy.

UX: confirmation gate by default; `--force` to skip; clear `--dry-run` output enumerating every artifact that would be touched. Follow existing Polyphony CLI verb conventions (see `polyphony-cli-developer` skill for ConsoleAppFramework + AOT JSON serialization patterns in `src/Polyphony/Commands/`).
title: polyphony reset --root-id verb with typed PolyphonyTag DU

acceptance_criteria: A second `Invoke-PolyphonySdlc.ps1 -ApexId N -Intent new` invocation against a work item carrying any `polyphony:*` state tag does not silently re-plan; it gates or auto-routes per the documented rule.
Tag detection consumes the `PolyphonyTag` DU from Issue 2; no ad-hoc string matching of `polyphony:*` exists in the driver.
Pester coverage for: tagged work item with `intent=new`, tagged work item with `intent=resume`, untagged work item with `intent=new`.
Warning/refusal message points to `polyphony reset --root-id N` as the path to a clean re-dispatch.
description:
Extend the apex-driver pre-flight (built in Issue 1) so that when the target work item carries any `polyphony:*` state tag — recognized via the `PolyphonyTag` DU from Issue 2 — the driver either:

- Surfaces a "previously processed" warning + decision gate to the operator, OR
- Auto-routes to `intent=resume` semantics when `intent=new` was passed.

Pick the routing rule based on which intent was supplied; document the chosen behaviour in the refusal/warning message. The check must use the typed `PolyphonyTag` DU rather than ad-hoc string matching, so the set of "polyphony-owned" tags has exactly one definition shared with the reset verb.
title: Apex-driver pre-flight tag check for previously-processed work items

acceptance_criteria: Apex dispatch computes the zero-diff condition against the configured base before invoking any planner/impl/MG sub-workflow.
When the condition holds and requirements are satisfied, dispatch routes to a process-config-defined terminal state with operator-visible signal — never silently succeeds.
No state name is hard-coded; the terminal state is sourced from `.polyphony-config/process-config.yaml` (P5).
AB#3127's reproducer (re-dispatch on a root whose `feature/N` already contains the merged fix) terminates cleanly and does not reach the scope reviewer.
Harness coverage under `tests/harness/scenarios/` exercises the zero-diff branch.
description:
At apex dispatch time — before any plan/impl/MG work — compute `git rev-list --count {base}..feature/{root}` against the configured base (typically `main`). If the count is zero AND the work item is non-terminal AND its requirements are otherwise satisfied, route directly to `terminal_apex_already_satisfied` (or the equivalent existing terminal state per `.polyphony-config/process-config.yaml` — do NOT hard-code state names; respect P5).

This closes the AB#3127 reproducer at the apex level: an empty MG never reaches the reviewer, so the false-positive `empty_merge_group_structural_violation` at `scope_revise_cap_gate` cannot fire from this cause.

The short-circuit must emit a distinct, operator-visible terminal state — not a silent success — so a future regression that produces a no-op impl is observable rather than masked.
title: Branch-tip zero-diff short-circuit at apex dispatch

acceptance_criteria: Scenario double-dispatches on the same root using the harness `FakeProvider`.
Scenario asserts the second run terminates in a known-good state — either the zero-diff short-circuit terminal state from Issue 4 or an artifact set identical to run 1.
Scenario fails loudly if the second run reaches `scope_revise_cap_gate` (the AB#3127 failure mode).
Scenario follows existing layout/conventions under `tests/harness/scenarios/`.
description:
Add a `tests/harness/` scenario that runs the apex driver twice on the same root and asserts the second run either short-circuits cleanly (per Issue 4) or produces an identical artifact set to the first. Worthwhile as a regression-prevention net but lower-leverage than Issues 1–4; sequence after they land.

See the `polyphony-harness` skill for scenario-authoring conventions (FakeProvider scripted LLM boundary, .NET shim for `script:` nodes, scenario layout under `tests/harness/scenarios/`).
title: Idempotency invariant harness scenario (deferred)
